work_unit: H-001 research_cache slug path traversal fix
situation: audit-2026-07-06-022 H-001: research_cache.py uses user-supplied slug directly in f-string path construction (_CACHE_DIR / f'{slug}.json') at lines 115, 165, 184. A slug like '../../etc/passwd' enables arbitrary file read/write outside the cache directory.
action: Added _SLUG_RE = re.compile(r'^[a-z0-9_-]+$') and _validate_slug() gate function. Called at entry of cmd_get, cmd_put, cmd_diff before any path construction. Invalid slugs print error to stderr and exit 0 (per tool script convention). Verified: traversal slug rejected, valid slug works.
outcome_evidence: python3 scripts/research_cache.py get '../../etc/passwd' → stderr '错误：slug 仅允许 [a-z0-9_-]'; python3 scripts/research_cache.py get 'valid-slug_01' → stdout '{"cached": false}'; commit pushed to origin/auto/opt-research-cache-1783343255
next_suggestion: Dispatch H-002 pipeline.py:222 shell injection via state['min_bytes'] f-string — pass via env dict

audit_findings.finding: slug used unsanitized in f-string path construction enabling path traversal
audit_findings.remediation: Validate slug against ^[a-z0-9_-]+$ regex before path use
